title: GitLab Runner Docker Machine / Autoscaler Provisioning Errors
dataSources: GitLab Runner logs (`sourcetype=gitlab:runner`) forwarded via Universal Forwarder; optional merge with `gitlab:pipeline`
app: Universal Forwarder on runner hosts; GitLab Runner file log or Docker logging driver → Splunk
spl:
index=devops sourcetype="gitlab:runner" OR sourcetype="gitlab:pipeline"
| search "machine creation" OR "autoscaler" OR "Failed to create" OR "docker-machine"
| stats count by runner_token_fingerprint, runner_host, coalesce(project_path, "unknown")
| where count >= 5
| sort -count
description: Provisioning errors starve CI capacity and lengthen queue times across teams sharing runners.
value: Identifies runner pools that cannot spawn executors so jobs backlog before developers notice queue SLA breaches.
implementation: Collect `/var/log/gitlab-runner.log` or container stdout; extract runner identity and error strings. Correlate with cloud quota and image pull errors.
visualization: Line chart (errors over time), Table (runner × project_path).
knownFalsePositives: Activity shifts during holidays, release freezes, or vendor maintenance; confirm against your change calendar.
grandmaExplanation: We watch GitLab runner and pipeline signals so broken jobs, storage, or runners do not stall your teams.
